content.help: How the body is pinned to the camera. Head-pin (default) glues the head node to the eye for a natural look-down; Root XY-pin holds a fixed horizontal offset and is the most pitch-stable; Off disables the body-to-camera anchor entirely.
Scales the visible body. Shrink it if you push the body low/forward and the feet clip through the floor.
Move the body forward (+) or back (-) while standing with your weapon sheathed.
Move the body right (+) or left (-) while standing with your weapon sheathed.
Move the body up (+) or down (-) while standing with your weapon sheathed.
Move the body forward (+) or back (-) while standing with your weapon drawn.
Move the body right (+) or left (-) while standing with your weapon drawn.
Move the body up (+) or down (-) while standing with your weapon drawn.
Move the body forward (+) or back (-) while sneaking with your weapon sheathed.
Move the body right (+) or left (-) while sneaking with your weapon sheathed.
Move the body up (+) or down (-) while sneaking with your weapon sheathed.
Move the body forward (+) or back (-) while sneaking with your weapon drawn.
Move the body right (+) or left (-) while sneaking with your weapon drawn.
Move the body up (+) or down (-) while sneaking with your weapon drawn.

content.text: Placeholder page — features are added over time.
content.help: Each toggle forces first person in a state that is normally third person. The switches save and persist now, but the behavior behind each one is still being built and turns on as it lands. Hover a toggle to see what it will cover.
Covers chairs, benches, thrones, and other furniture. When on, forces first person while seated. Coming soon — the toggle persists but is not active yet.
Covers forge, tanning rack, smelter, alchemy, enchanting, and cooking. When on, forces first person at crafting stations. Coming soon — the toggle persists but is not active yet.
Covers riding a horse. When on, forces first person while mounted. Coming soon — the toggle persists but is not active yet.
Covers the Werewolf and Vampire Lord transformations. When on, forces first person while transformed. Coming soon — the toggle persists but is not active yet.
Covers melee and ranged killmoves. Leave OFF to keep cinematic third-person kills (e.g. from Violens) untouched. Coming soon — the toggle persists but is not active yet.

Body offsets for the Werewolf and Vampire Lord forms, for the future feature that forces first person while transformed (see Feature Toggles). Each form is a different skeleton, so each has its own trims. These values save now but do nothing yet — the transform body-ride is not built.
Move the Werewolf body forward (+) or back (-). Not yet applied.
Move the Werewolf body right (+) or left (-). Not yet applied.
Move the Werewolf body up (+) or down (-). Not yet applied.
Move the Vampire Lord body forward (+) or back (-). Not yet applied.
Move the Vampire Lord body right (+) or left (-). Not yet applied.
Move the Vampire Lord body up (+) or down (-). Not yet applied.

OFF by default so Embody's keys can't clash with other mods or vanilla UI. Turn ON to tune the body live with the keys below; the bindings below are greyed out until you do. Everything the hotkeys do is also available on the Body Tuning page, so you can leave this off.

Click a binding, then press a key to rebind — changes apply immediately (the plugin reads the new scan code on the fly). The keys only work while 'Enable Tuning Hotkeys' is on; body-position keys act on the stance you are currently in.
Write the current live-tuned values to the config file so they persist across launches.
Cycle the anchor mode: Off -> Head-pin -> Root XY-pin -> Off.
Nudge the body forward in the current stance.
Nudge the body back in the current stance.
Nudge the body left in the current stance.
Nudge the body right in the current stance.
Nudge the body up in the current stance.
Nudge the body down in the current stance.
Grow the visible body by 1%.
Shrink the visible body by 1%.
